name: Transition State on the PES
description: Locating a reaction pathway on the potential energy surface: build an initial path between reactant and product, optimize it (e.g. nudged elastic band) to a first-order saddle point, verify it has a single imaginary frequency, and confirm by IRC before reading the activation energy.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Reactant + product geom"]
    N2["Build initial path (NEB)"]
    N3["Optimize images on PES"]
    N4["Locate saddle point (TS)"]
    N5{"One imaginary frequency?"}
    N6["Refine TS guess"]
    N7{"IRC connects R and P?"}
    N8["Re-search the path"]
    N9["Activation energy Ea"]
    N10["Validated pathway"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 -->|no| N6
    N6 -->|refine| N4
    N5 -->|yes| N7
    N7 -->|no| N8
    N8 -->|restart| N2
    N7 -->|yes| N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: A climbing image nudged elastic band method for finding saddle points
sources.authors: Henkelman, G.; Uberuaga, B. P.; Jónsson, H.

sources.title: The intrinsic reaction coordinate. An ab initio calculation
Introduction to Computational Chemistry, 3rd ed. — Optimization and Reaction Paths

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.
